content.problemStatement:
Consider a set of points in 2D which is initially empty.
You need to process queries which insert and remove points from it.

After each query, you should also print the square of the distance between two closest points in the set.
If there are fewer than 2 points in the set, print 0 instead.

 It is guaranteed that all the queries are valid — there will be no query of adding a point that is already present in the set or
removing a point which does not exist in the set.
content.inputFormat: The first line of the input contains an integer Q denoting the number of queries. Each of the next Q lines contains one query, which will be one of the following two types: + x y — add the point (x, y) to the set, - x y — remove the point (x, y) from the set.
content.outputFormat: For each query, output a single line containing one integer — the square of the distance between two closest points in the set. If there are fewer than 2 points in the set, print 0 instead.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Query 1. There is only 1 point, so the answer is 0 
Query 2. There are only 2 points, (1, 1) and (2, 2), so answer is (1 - 2)^2 + (1 - 2)^2, which equals to 2 
Query 3. The points (1, 1) and (2, 2) are one of the closest pairs, and so the answer is still 2 
Query 4. There are only 2 points, (1, 1) and (3, 3), and so answer is (1 - 3)^2 + (1 - 3)^2 which equals to 8 
Query 5. Points (1, 1) and (3, 3) are still closest, and so answer is 8  
Query 6. There are only 2 points, (3, 3) and (400, 400), and so answer is (3 - 400)^2 + (3 - 400)^2 equals to 
315218

solution.approach: To solve this problem, we can use a brute force approach by calculating the distance between each pair of points after each query. We will maintain a set of points and update it according to the queries. For each query, we will calculate the square of the distance between each pair of points and keep track of the minimum distance found so far.
solution.timeComplexity: O(Q * N^2) — where Q is the number of queries and N is the maximum number of points in the set at any time. This is because for each query, we are potentially calculating the distance between each pair of points.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N) — where N is the maximum number of points in the set at any time. This is because we need to store all the points in the set.
